lead.title: Cloudflare Cuts 1,100 Jobs at Record Revenue
lead.standfirst: Cloudflare announced its first mass layoff in sixteen years on May 7 — 1,100 jobs eliminated alongside record quarterly revenue — and explicitly attributed the cut to internal AI agent adoption. The move is the clearest disclosed case yet of AI displacement at a growing company.

secondary.title: Westminster Briefing — 11 May 2026: Starmer's Survival Week
secondary.standfirst: Sir Keir Starmer survives Monday without an immediate leadership challenge but with 77 Labour MPs publicly demanding his resignation timetable. The reset speech committed to British Steel nationalisation and a Europe-facing reset; gilts sold off regardless.

secondary.title: NBA Conference Semifinals — 11 May 2026
secondary.standfirst: The Knicks complete a four-game sweep of the 76ers behind a postseason three-point barrage; Donovan Mitchell ties an NBA playoff record with 39 second-half points to drag Cleveland back to 2-2 against Detroit. The Thunder were in Los Angeles overnight chasing a sweep of the Lakers.

secondary.title: Westminster Briefing: 10 May 2026
secondary.standfirst: Reform surges in English local elections as Starmer faces leadership pressure; King's Speech legislative programme previewed ahead of Tuesday's state opening.

further_reading.title: Wall Street's AI Chip Rotation
further_reading.standfirst: AMD, Intel, and Micron have outrun Nvidia by orders of magnitude year-to-date as Wall Street rotates AI-hardware capital from training GPUs to inference-era CPUs and memory. Nvidia's counter-move is a 0 billion equity-investment campaign across its own customer base.

further_reading.title: Warsh Clears Cloture as Partisan Fed Confirmation Concludes
further_reading.standfirst: The Senate's Monday cloture vote on Kevin Warsh's Fed nomination — 49-44 on party lines — caps the most strictly partisan confirmation process in Fed Chair history. Warsh inherits a Fed pinned between an Iran-war energy shock and a shutdown-distorted core inflation print due Tuesday.

further_reading.title: Hormuz Under Fire
further_reading.standfirst: US fires on Iranian tankers as Hormuz talks stall; ceasefire holds nominally. A US Navy F/A-18 disabled two Iranian-flagged tankers attempting to breach the naval blockade. Iran has yet to respond to a 14-point MOU proposed by Washington.

further_reading.title: NBA Playoffs: 10 May 2026
further_reading.standfirst: Thunder go 3-0 over the Lakers as Ajay Mitchell posts 24 points and 10 assists with zero turnovers. Oklahoma City are 7-0 in these playoffs.

further_reading.title: Trade Court Strikes Down Trump's Section 122 Tariffs
further_reading.standfirst: A 2-1 panel of the US Court of International Trade ruled on May 7 that the administration's 10% global import surcharge exceeds presidential authority under Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 — but limited relief to three named plaintiffs, leaving the surcharge in place for all other US importers.

further_reading.title: China's Legal Shield
further_reading.standfirst: China's Ministry of Commerce invoked its Blocking Rules for the first time on 2 May, ordering domestic firms not to comply with US sanctions on five Chinese oil refineries. The legal escalation transforms a bilateral diplomatic dispute into a multilateral compliance problem for any international bank or shipper caught between Washington and Beijing.

further_reading.title: White House Reverses on AI Oversight
further_reading.standfirst: An administration that spent its first year opposing AI oversight is now drafting an executive order requiring pre-deployment government vetting of advanced models, following the Mythos security alarm. The reversal signals that national security framing is doing what the broader AI safety debate failed to do.

further_reading.title: The Pharma Tariff Tier
further_reading.standfirst: Trump's Section 232 pharmaceutical tariffs impose 100% on patented drug imports from 17 named companies, with a July 31 deadline. The tiered structure — 100% default, 20% for approved onshoring plans, zero for MFN holders — creates a compliance landscape almost entirely absent from mainstream coverage.
